It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== References == {{Reflist}}

== External links == {{Commonscat|Uchima Udun}} * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/27uchimagoden.html Uchima Udun - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/15senohkyutakuhi.html Former Residence Monument of the Late King - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * {{Database of Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|401|00003707|Uchima Udun}}

It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).

It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.

At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.

"Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.

It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).

It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.

As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.

In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.

'''Uchima Udun''' (内間御殿) is a sacred site in [[:en:Nishihara, Okinawa|Nishihara, Okinawa]], [[:en:Nakagami District, Okinawa|Nakagami District, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.

It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). === Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.

It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).

Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.

It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).
== Overview == Uchima Udun was established in [[:en:1666|1666]], 190 years after the death of [[:en:Shō En|King Shō En]], the founder of the [[:en:Second Shō dynasty|Second Shō Dynasty]]. It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.

In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.

"Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also == * [[List of Historic Sites of Kyushu and Okinawa]]

It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).
== Overview == Uchima Udun was established in [[:en:1666|1666]], 190 years after the death of [[:en:Shō En|King Shō En]], the founder of the [[:en:Second Shō dynasty|Second Shō Dynasty]]. It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q56346970]] Wikidata name: Uchima Udun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Uchima Udun |image=File:Agari Udun, Uchima Udun at Nishihara 202410.jpg|thumb|caption=Agari Udun, part of Uchima Udun }}
'''Uchima Udun''' (内間御殿) is a sacred site in [[:en:Nishihara, Okinawa|Nishihara, Okinawa]], [[:en:Nakagami District, Okinawa|Nakagami District, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).
== Overview == Uchima Udun was established in [[:en:1666|1666]], 190 years after the death of [[:en:Shō En|King Shō En]], the founder of the [[:en:Second Shō dynasty|Second Shō Dynasty]]. It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also == * [[List of Historic Sites of Kyushu and Okinawa]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Uchima Udun}} * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/27uchimagoden.html Uchima Udun - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/15senohkyutakuhi.html Former Residence Monument of the Late King - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * {{Database of Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|401|00003707|Uchima Udun}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q56346970]] Wikidata name: Uchima Udun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Uchima Udun |image=File:Agari Udun, Uchima Udun at Nishihara 202410.jpg|thumb|caption=Agari Udun, part of Uchima Udun }}
'''Uchima Udun''' (内間御殿) is a sacred site in [[:en:Nishihara, Okinawa|Nishihara, Okinawa]], [[:en:Nakagami District, Okinawa|Nakagami District, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).
== Overview == Uchima Udun was established in [[:en:1666|1666]], 190 years after the death of [[:en:Shō En|King Shō En]], the founder of the [[:en:Second Shō dynasty|Second Shō Dynasty]]. It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also == * [[List of Historic Sites of Kyushu and Okinawa]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Uchima Udun}} * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/27uchimagoden.html Uchima Udun - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/15senohkyutakuhi.html Former Residence Monument of the Late King - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * {{Database of Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|401|00003707|Uchima Udun}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q56346970]] Wikidata name: Uchima Udun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Uchima Udun |image=File:Agari Udun, Uchima Udun at Nishihara 202410.jpg|thumb|caption=Agari Udun, part of Uchima Udun }}
'''Uchima Udun''' (内間御殿) is a sacred site in [[:en:Nishihara, Okinawa|Nishihara, Okinawa]], [[:en:Nakagami District, Okinawa|Nakagami District, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).
== Overview == Uchima Udun was established in [[:en:1666|1666]], 190 years after the death of [[:en:Shō En|King Shō En]], the founder of the [[:en:Second Shō dynasty|Second Shō Dynasty]]. It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also == * [[List of Historic Sites of Kyushu and Okinawa]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Uchima Udun}} * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/27uchimagoden.html Uchima Udun - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/15senohkyutakuhi.html Former Residence Monument of the Late King - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * {{Database of Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|401|00003707|Uchima Udun}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Uchima Udun]][[en:Uchimaudūn]][[ja:内間御殿]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q56346970]] Wikidata name: Uchima Udun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |name=Uchima Udun |image=File:Agari Udun, Uchima Udun at Nishihara 202410.jpg|thumb|caption=Agari Udun, part of Uchima Udun }}
'''Uchima Udun''' (内間御殿) is a sacred site in [[:en:Nishihara, Okinawa|Nishihara, Okinawa]], [[:en:Nakagami District, Okinawa|Nakagami District, Okinawa]], [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]]. It was designated a [[Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] on [[February 7, 2011]] (Heisei 23).
== Overview == Uchima Udun was established in [[:en:1666|1666]], 190 years after the death of [[:en:Shō En|King Shō En]], the founder of the [[:en:Second Shō dynasty|Second Shō Dynasty]]. It was built on the former residence of Kanamaru (later King Shō En) when he was appointed chieftain of Uchima<ref name="report">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2012). "Uchima Udun: National Designation Symposium Report," pp. 3-5</ref><ref name="choshi4">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1989). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 4: Folklore of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 260-261</ref>. Uchima Udun consists of the {{ill|Seie Udun|ja|西江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seie Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the north and the {{ill|Agari Udun|ja|東江御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agari Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south<ref name="choshi5">Nishihara Town History Compilation Committee (1999). "Nishihara Town History, Volume 5: Archaeological Materials of Nishihara," Nishihara Town Office, pp. 169-170</ref>.
Kanamaru was a vassal of {{ill|Prince Gushichan|ja|越来王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Gushichan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later [[:en:Shō Taikyū|King Shō Taikyū]]) and was appointed chieftain of Uchima in [[:en:1454|1454]]<ref name="report" />. During this time, he took the younger sister of Kamuta Shinmuta of Kadekaru as a concubine<ref name="choshi4"></ref>. As a result, the former residence of Kanamaru came to be called {{ill|Ōdonuchi|ja|大殿内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdonuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="report" />.
== Location == * Uchima Udun is located at 42-1, 50, and 51 Uenomatsu, Kadekaru, Nishihara Town, Nakagami District, Okinawa Prefecture.
== History == === Establishment === In 1666, on the advice of the then-[[Regent (Japan)|Regent]] [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]] (Shōken), a thatched {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} shrine (2 ken × 3 ken) called Agari Udun was built at the site of the Ōdonuchi residence<ref name="report" />. At this time, a [[:en:Celadon|celadon]] pillow was enshrined as the sacred object<ref name="choshi5" />. Originally, Agari Udun was surrounded by bamboo fencing rather than stone walls<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== Designation as a Sacred Site by the Royal Government === In [[:en:1689|1689]], the {{ill|Ōmii Udun|ja|大美御殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmii Udun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the government office overseeing Ryukyuan royal rituals, reconstructed the damaged Agari Udun using oak wood and converted it into a tiled shrine<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />. This officially recognized Uchima Udun as a sacred site connected to the Ryukyu Kingdom, leading to further government maintenance<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1706|1706]], the residents of Nishihara Magiri built a thatched shrine (3 ken × 2.5 ken) north of Uchima Udun, later known as "Seie Udun."
=== Theft of the Treasure Pillow and Fortification === In [[:en:1735|1735]], the treasured sacred pillow was stolen. In response, the following year ([[:en:1736|1736]]), the bamboo fencing was replaced with stone walls, and main and side gates were installed, featuring [[:en:bolt (fastener)|bolt locks]]. The shrine was also tiled<ref name="choshi4" /><ref name="choshi5" />.
In [[:en:1737|1737]], Seie Udun was also converted into a tiled structure, with bamboo fencing added around its perimeter<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1738|1738]], King [[:en:Shō Kei|Shō Kei]] inscribed the plaque "Chiwa" (致和), which was placed above the main gate of Agari Udun<ref name="bunkazai">Nishihara Town Board of Education (2013). "Cultural Properties of Nishihara Town," pp. 6-7</ref>. A stone monument called "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" (先王旧宅碑), detailing the history of the renovation, was erected in the inner courtyard<ref name="choshi4" />.
=== The Battle of Okinawa and Restoration === During the final stages of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1945|1945]], both shrines were destroyed in the [[:en:Battle of Okinawa|Battle of Okinawa]], and the "Monument of the Former Residence of the Late King" was partially damaged by shellfire<ref name="report" />.
In [[:en:1951|1951]], donations from the {{ill|Ōya Monchu|ja|大屋門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of the Ōdonuchi family) and Ryukyuan emigrants to [[:en:Hawaii|Hawaii]] led to the reconstruction of Agari Udun with a corrugated iron-roofed shrine<ref name="report" />. Seie Udun was reconstructed in thatched form by the {{ill|Irei Monchu|ja|伊礼門中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irei Monchu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the original caretakers of the site<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
In [[:en:1960|1960]], Agari Udun was renovated into a wooden structure with a corrugated iron roof (2 ken × 2 ken)<ref name="report" />. In [[:en:1974|1974]], it was rebuilt in its current form using concrete blocks (2 ken × 2.5 ken) by Masao Nakayama, the head of the Ōya Monchu<ref name="bunkazai" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== The Sacred Pillow == In 1735, thieves broke into Agari Udun and stole the treasured sacred pillow. King Shō Kei personally led his retainers in searching for the item, ultimately recovering it from a rice field<ref name="bunkazai" />.
However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also == * [[List of Historic Sites of Kyushu and Okinawa]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Uchima Udun}} * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/27uchimagoden.html Uchima Udun - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/15senohkyutakuhi.html Former Residence Monument of the Late King - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * {{Database of Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|401|00003707|Uchima Udun}}

However, in [[:en:1842|1842]], another burglary occurred, and the sacred pillow was stolen once again, never to be recovered<ref name="report" />. As a result, in [[:en:1835|1835]], a celadon dish enshrined in Seie Udun was designated as the new sacred object of Agari Udun<ref name="report" /><ref name="choshi4" />.

== See also == * [[List of Historic Sites of Kyushu and Okinawa]]
== External links == {{Commonscat|Uchima Udun}} * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/27uchimagoden.html Uchima Udun - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * [http://www.town.nishihara.okinawa.jp/asset/15senohkyutakuhi.html Former Residence Monument of the Late King - Cultural Property of Nishihara Town] * {{Database of Nationally Designated Cultural Properties|401|00003707|Uchima Udun}}
